extension.description: Pin files as favorites and run scripts. Single-click opens, double-click executes.
views.container.title: Saropa Workspace
views.pins.name: Pins
views.recipes.name: Recipes
views.projectFiles.name: Project Files
viewsWelcome.pins.contents:
No pins yet.
Pin a file from the editor title menu or the Explorer right-click menu, then single-click to open it or double-click to run it.
[Pin the active file](command:saropaWorkspace.pinActiveFile)
viewsWelcome.recipes.contents:
No recipes detected.
Recipes are shortcuts auto-detected from your project (open on GitHub, run scripts, Saropa Suite tools). They appear here when this workspace has files like .git/config, package.json, or a Makefile.
viewsWelcome.projectFiles.contents:
No recognized project files found in this workspace.
This view lists files like README, CHANGELOG, and package manifests with their last-modified time and version.

config.autoPins.patterns.description: Filenames or globs auto-pinned per project (removable). Removing an auto-pin keeps it removed.
config.doubleClickMs.description: Window in milliseconds for detecting a double-click on a pin. A second click within this window runs the file; a single click opens it.
config.previewMode.enabled.description: When on, single-clicking a pinned file opens it in a transient Preview tab (italic title). Clicking another pinned file reuses that tab, so clicking through several reference files does not flood the editor with tabs. Editing the file, or double-clicking a non-runnable pin, promotes the tab to permanent. Off by default: pins open as permanent tabs.
config.defaultUseIntegratedTerminal.desc: Run pinned scripts in the integrated terminal by default (so output is visible). When off, runs in the background and streams to an output channel.
config.terminalName.description: Name of the reused integrated terminal for pinned scripts.
config.interpreterDefaults.description: Default command prefix per file extension, used when a pin has no explicit command set. An explicit per-pin command always wins.
config.suggestions.enabled.description: Offer to pin a file you open often. Counts are kept on this machine only and never transmitted. Each file is offered at most once.
config.suggestions.openThreshold.descrip: How many times a file must be opened before Saropa Workspace offers to pin it.
config.projectFiles.enabled.description: Show the Project Files view, listing files like README, CHANGELOG, and package manifests with their last-modified time and declared version.
config.recipes.enabled.description: Show auto-detected recipes (URL, run, command, and macro pins derived from project files such as .git/config, package.json, and Makefile) in a Recipes group. Removing a recipe keeps it removed; restore them with Restore Recipes.
config.aiContext.enabled.description: Scan the configured chat folders for active Claude/AI conversations and surface the most recent ones as pins in an Active AI Threads group. Removing a thread keeps it removed; restore them with Restore Recipes.
config.aiContext.claudeChatFolders.descr: Workspace-relative folders scanned for Claude/AI chat transcripts (.md or .json). The 10 most recently modified files per scan are offered as pins: a Markdown file opens locally (or deep-links to claude.ai when it carries a chat URL), a JSON file deep-links to claude.ai by its id. Each folder is scanned at its top level only, never recursively.
config.telemetry.enabled.description: Keep a local, on-device record of pin runs (manual and scheduled) to power the Recent group and the recents in Run Pin. Stored on this machine only and never transmitted. Turn off to stop collecting; use Reset Run History to clear it.
config.projectFiles.files.description: Root-relative file names surfaced in the Project Files view. Each is shown only when it exists. Version is read from package.json, pubspec.yaml, Cargo.toml, pyproject.toml, and the top entry of CHANGELOG.md.
Sample your detected toolchain's processes on a timer while a workspace is open, appending a per-tool CPU/RAM/process-count row to reports/process-trend.csv and warning only when a tool crosses a RAM or helper-count ceiling. Off by default; the live monitor and snapshot work without it. Nothing is transmitted, and the heartbeat never ends a process.
config.processMonitor.heartbeat.interval: How often the toolchain heartbeat samples, in minutes (when enabled).
config.processMonitor.ramCeilingMB.descr: Per-tool working-set RAM ceiling, in megabytes. The heartbeat warns once when a tool (e.g. a leaked analysis server) exceeds it, and again only after it drops back under.
config.processMonitor.helperCountCeiling: Per-tool helper-process count ceiling. The heartbeat warns once when a tool (e.g. an editor helper swarm) exceeds it, and again only after it drops back under.
config.hygiene.mode.description: What the hygiene scan reports: empty (zero-byte files and zero-child folders), oversized (files and folders past the size ceilings), or both.
config.hygiene.fileMaxMB.description: Hygiene scan: a file larger than this many megabytes is reported as oversized.
config.hygiene.folderMaxMB.description: Hygiene scan: a folder whose recursive total exceeds this many megabytes is reported as oversized.
config.hygiene.fileMinMB.description: Hygiene scan: when greater than 0, a non-empty file smaller than this many megabytes is reported as under-size. 0 disables the floor check.
config.hygiene.respectGitignore.descript: Hygiene scan: read the project's top-level .gitignore and skip what it names (in addition to a built-in ignore set covering node_modules, .git, and build output). Turn off to scan ignored paths too.
config.hygiene.exclude.description: Hygiene scan: additional glob patterns to skip (matched against each entry's path relative to its scan root), e.g. "**/*.snap" or "coverage".
config.sound.enabled.description: Play a short audio cue when a pin run starts or finishes. Off by default; the cue is additive to the on-screen toast and uses your OS's built-in system sounds (so it follows the OS volume and mute). The per-event toggles below choose which moments chime, and a pin's Configure Run can override this for that pin.
config.sound.onStart.description: When sound is enabled, play a cue when a run starts. Off by default (start cues can be chatty).
config.sound.onSuccess.description: When sound is enabled, play a distinct success cue when a run finishes successfully. Background and report runs report an outcome; terminal and external-window runs do not, so they cue only on start.
config.sound.onFailure.description: When sound is enabled, play a distinct failure cue when a run exits with an error.
